name: Spicy Shortbread Bites
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a small bowl, whisk together paprika, 2 and 1/2 teaspoons salt, and 1 teaspoon cayenne. Set aside.
3. In a bowl with an electric mixer, beat butter with sugar until light and fluffy.
4. In another bowl, whisk together the remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/8 teaspoon cayenne, flour, curry powder, and ground cumin.
5. Beat the flour mixture into the butter mixture until just combined.
6. On a lightly floured surface, knead dough about 8 times or until it just comes together.
7. Divide dough into 4 equal pieces.
8. On a sheet of wax paper, roll each piece of dough into a 3/4-inch thick log.
9. Sprinkle one quarter of the spice mixture along the length of each log and roll in the mixture to coat completely.
10. Wrap each log in wax paper. Freeze dough just until firm, 15 to 20 minutes.
11. Shortbread dough may be made up to 1 week ahead and frozen.
12. Before proceeding with recipe, remove dough from freezer and let stand about 15 minutes or until slightly softened to facilitate slicing.
13. Remove logs carefully from wax paper and slice into 1/2-inch thick rounds.
14. Place rounds 1/4 inch apart on ungreased baking sheets and bake in batches in the middle of the oven for 15 minutes, or until browned lightly.
15. Gently loosen shortbread bites with a spatula from the baking sheet (do not remove from sheet) and cool completely.
16. Carefully transfer shortbread bites to a serving dish (they will be fragile) and serve as hors d'oeuvre. Makes about 140 shortbread bites.
